flavorText: Hunters adapt specialized tactics to stalk and eliminate dangerous prey.

features.description: Choose one option: Colossus Slayer (+1d8 damage to an already-damaged target), Giant Killer (reaction attack when a Large or larger creature misses you), or Horde Breaker (extra attack against a different adjacent creature).

Choose one option: Escape the Horde (Disengage avoids opportunity attacks), Multiattack Defense (+4 AC against subsequent attacks by same creature after first hit), or Steel Will (advantage on saves against being frightened).
Gain either Volley (ranged area attack) or Whirlwind Attack (melee attack against multiple nearby creatures).
When a hostile creature misses you, you can force that attack to target another creature (other than the attacker) of your choice.

hunter-hunters-prey.summary: Choose how you specialize in dropping prey so the subclass has a clear offensive identity from the start.
hunter-hunters-prey.description: Hunter’s Prey is the subclass fork that tells the sheet what kind of slayer this ranger is trying to be. It should feel like a real tactical choice, not a hidden setup detail.

hunter-defensive-tactics.summary: Your experience against dangerous creatures sharpens the defensive instincts that keep a hunter alive.
hunter-defensive-tactics.description: Defensive Tactics reinforces the subclass as a seasoned predator-hunter who survives by reading the fight correctly, not by standing still and trading.

hunter-multiattack.summary: Apply your hunting style across multiple foes or multiple strikes when the battlefield opens up.
hunter-multiattack.description: Multiattack is the subclass’s battlefield pressure spike. It should feel like a practiced technique for capitalizing on target-rich encounters.

hunter-stand-against-the-tide.summary: Turn an enemy’s momentum against them when they overcommit around you.
hunter-stand-against-the-tide.description: Stand Against the Tide is a veteran hunter trick—measured, punishing, and built around letting the foe make the wrong move first.
